title: Buttery Crispy Mexican Bean Stew
description: A velvety, ghee-rich bean stew with a burst of Mexican spices and a crunchy tortilla chip topping, brightened with a splash of red food coloring for that vibrant hue.
instructions: Heat the ghee in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced red bell pepper and sauté for 2 minutes until slightly softened.
Stir in the chopped tomato, cumin, coriander, chili powder, and paprika; cook for 3 minutes, letting the spices bloom.
Pour in the cooked black and pinto beans, corn, and a splash of water (about 1/2 cup) to loosen the stew; bring to a gentle simmer.
Add salt to taste and a few drops of red food coloring; stir to distribute the color evenly; continue simmering for 10 minutes to meld flavors.
Transfer the stew to individual bowls and top each with a sprinkle of crushed tortilla chips for a crispy texture.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
Serve hot, allowing the creamy base and crispy topping to contrast beautifully.
